title: rosa communis subsp. canina
intro: Rosa communis subsp. canina, also known as dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa communis plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe, western Asia, and northwest Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Rosa communis subsp. canina include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy, often in the form of tea, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions.
